description_long: This is a vintage military-style radio receiver with a rugged, boxy metal housing finished in olive drab green. The front panel features several large black rotary knobs, a couple of analog meters with glass covers, and a set of labeled switches and dials, some with Cyrillic script. There are also two circular speaker grilles on the left and right sides, each with vertical slats for sound output. A thick black power cord extends from the rear, and the front panel includes a small gauge and a multi-pin connector. The overall design is utilitarian, with exposed screws and a robust, functional appearance typical of mid-20th-century field equipment.
description: A rugged, olive green vintage radio receiver with multiple rotary knobs, analog meters, and side speaker grilles. The front panel is detailed with Cyrillic labels and a utilitarian design.
description_view_0: The front view shows the main control panel with rotary knobs, analog meters, labeled switches, and a multi-pin connector.
description_view_1: The left side view displays a prominent speaker grille and the protruding ends of some front panel knobs.
description_view_2: The rear view is mostly plain, showing the olive green casing and a thick black power cord extending from the bottom right.
description_view_3: The right side view mirrors the left, featuring another speaker grille and the ends of the front panel controls.
